classDescription: A wilderness hunter and skirmisher with primal magic.
roleIdentity: Mobile striker and scout with sustained utility and control tools.

ranger-favored-enemy.summary: Mark dangerous prey and study enemy patterns so your damage and tracking feel purposeful rather than generic.
ranger-favored-enemy.description: Favored Enemy should make the ranger feel like a hunter with intent. It is about target pressure, tracking, and tactical focus instead of simply being a passive lore note.
ranger-favored-enemy.effect: Hunter's Mark support, target pressure, and tracking identity.

ranger-weapon-mastery.summary: Your chosen weapons carry tactical mastery properties that make attacks feel more distinctive than simple swings.
ranger-weapon-mastery.description: Weapon Mastery is an attack-surface feature. It matters because it changes how the ranger approaches weapon turns and should be visible wherever weapon actions are shown.
ranger-weapon-mastery.effect: Choose mastered weapons and use mastery properties through weapon attack cards.

ranger-deft-explorer.summary: You become a stronger explorer and traveler so the ranger contributes clearly between fights as well as during them.
ranger-deft-explorer.description: Deft Explorer is utility identity for ranger. It should help the class feel like a guide, scout, and endurance specialist instead of only a bow user with spells.

ranger-spellcasting.summary: Half-caster spell progression supports your martial hunt loop with practical field magic.
ranger-spellcasting.description: Ranger spellcasting should present clear slot and known-spell growth, Wisdom scaling, and utility/control options that complement weapon turns.
ranger-spellcasting.effect: Wisdom-based spellcasting with delayed half-caster slot cadence.

ranger-fighting-style.summary: Pick a permanent fighting posture so your ranger’s ranged, melee, or hybrid plan is explicit.
ranger-fighting-style.description: Fighting Style is a structural class choice. It should clearly influence whether the ranger reads as ranged pressure, defensive skirmisher, two-weapon attacker, or another martial angle.

choices.name: Archery
choices.description: +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

choices.name: Defense
choices.description: +1 AC while you are wearing armor.

choices.name: Dueling
choices.description: +2 damage when fighting with a single one-handed melee weapon.

choices.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
choices.description: Add your ability modifier to the damage of your off-hand attack.

choices.name: Blind Fighting
choices.description: Gain blindsight in a short range to fight through darkness or concealment.

choices.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
choices.description: Draw thrown weapons freely and improve thrown-weapon damage.

ranger-conclave-subclass.summary: Your conclave determines how you hunt, survive, and shape your battlefield presence as the class progresses.
ranger-conclave-subclass.description: Ranger Conclave is the subclass gate for ranger. It should instantly tell the player whether this character is a stalker, companion master, focused slayer, fey wanderer, or another specialist hunter.

ranger-roving.summary: Your movement becomes more fluid, letting you cross the field and rough terrain like a practiced scout.
ranger-roving.description: Roving is a quality-of-life mobility feature, but it matters constantly because it improves how the ranger approaches pursuit, kiting, and difficult approaches.

ranger-tireless.summary: You can shake off fatigue and keep moving, reinforcing the ranger as a long-haul survivor.
ranger-tireless.description: Tireless is an endurance feature. It should matter on travel-heavy days, attrition-heavy adventuring, and any campaign where the ranger’s staying power matters as much as their aim.

ranger-natures-veil.summary: Slip from sight with primal subtlety so you can reposition, disengage attention, or open a cleaner line.
ranger-natures-veil.description: Nature’s Veil is a strong tactical button. It helps the ranger take control of line-of-sight and positioning, which is exactly where the class often wants to fight.

ranger-foe-slayer.summary: Your greatest hunts end with deadly certainty, turning focused pressure into a true capstone threat.
ranger-foe-slayer.description: Foe Slayer is the ranger capstone identity and a true hunter payoff: when the quarry matters, the class becomes precise and deadly enough to finish the hunt with real certainty.

ranger-asi-4.summary: Increase key abilities or take a feat to sharpen either your martial plan or field utility.
ranger-asi-4.description: Ranger ASI levels are where you lock in whether this character leans harder into weapon pressure, spell reliability, or broad utility.

ranger-extra-attack.summary: Your Attack action now carries two strikes, making your martial turn reliable even when spells are reserved.
ranger-extra-attack.description: Extra Attack is the core martial cadence jump for ranger. This level should feel like a real damage and consistency spike for both melee and ranged builds.

ranger-subclass-feature-6.summary: Your conclave grants a mid-tier technique that deepens your chosen hunting style.
ranger-subclass-feature-6.description: Level 6 is where subclass identity stops being introductory flavor and starts changing your round-by-round decisions in a concrete way.

ranger-expertise-second-skill.summary: Gain deeper expertise in a second key skill to reinforce scouting and hunt reliability.
ranger-expertise-second-skill.description: This expertise pick helps ranger stay valuable outside initiative by locking in one more signature competency such as Stealth, Perception, or Survival.

ranger-conjure-barrage.summary: Fire a limited-use area burst when single-target hunting is not enough.
ranger-conjure-barrage.description: Conjure Barrage gives ranger a true clustered-enemy answer and should appear as an active tactical button, not buried passive text.

ranger-asi-8.summary: Another ASI/feat breakpoint to reinforce your chosen hunting posture.
ranger-asi-8.description: At this tier, ASI decisions should connect directly to your active plan: accuracy, durability, spell reliability, or mobility.

ranger-lands-stride.summary: Ignore many terrain penalties so pursuit and repositioning stay in your control.
ranger-lands-stride.description: Land’s Stride is a movement identity feature that keeps ranger fast through hostile terrain and reinforces skirmisher/scout pacing.

ranger-third-level-slots.summary: You unlock 3rd-level spell slots, expanding half-caster impact without replacing your weapon plan.
ranger-third-level-slots.description: This is a half-caster milestone: stronger spell options arrive on a delayed cadence while martial pressure remains central.

ranger-subclass-feature-10.summary: Your conclave grants an advanced tactic that sharpens your subclass lane in live play.
ranger-subclass-feature-10.description: By level 10, subclass tools should feel mature and immediately recognizable on the sheet and in actions/features surfaces.

ranger-conjure-volley.summary: Unleash a larger limited-use volley to punish clustered enemies at higher tier.
ranger-conjure-volley.description: Conjure Volley is a signature ranger crowd-pressure button and should read as a real encounter swing option with clear usage limits.

ranger-asi-12.summary: Increase core stats or take a feat to keep your hunt loop scaling cleanly.
ranger-asi-12.description: This ASI/feat level lets ranger tune precision, endurance, or spell reliability for mid-tier encounters.

ranger-fourth-level-slots.summary: You unlock 4th-level spell slots on the ranger half-caster timeline.
ranger-fourth-level-slots.description: This progression point should make the delayed-but-steady spell growth obvious so ranger does not read like a vague fighter-plus-magic mashup.

ranger-subclass-feature-14.summary: Your conclave grants a high-tier feature that defines late-game identity.
ranger-subclass-feature-14.description: This unlock is where subclass differentiation should be unmistakable across Hunter, Beast Master, and Gloom Stalker.

ranger-conjure-barrage-improvement.summary: Your barrage package scales so area pressure remains relevant in higher-tier combat.
ranger-conjure-barrage-improvement.description: This improvement keeps ranger’s limited-use area tools competitive instead of falling behind single-target routines.

ranger-asi-16.summary: Late-tier ASI/feat choice to finalize your endgame hunting build.
ranger-asi-16.description: Use this breakpoint to close gaps in durability, save DC reliability, initiative, or weapon accuracy depending on your campaign pressure.

ranger-fifth-level-slots.summary: You unlock 5th-level spell slots, completing ranger’s half-caster spell tier ladder.
ranger-fifth-level-slots.description: This capstone slot tier should read as a meaningful upgrade while still preserving ranger’s martial-first identity.

ranger-feral-senses.summary: You remain aware of elusive threats, keeping pressure on hidden or slippery prey.
ranger-feral-senses.description: Feral Senses reinforces the hunter fantasy at high levels by reducing how often concealment tricks shut down your offense.

ranger-epic-boon.summary: Gain an epic boon-style endgame enhancement on top of your ranger kit.
ranger-epic-boon.description: This late progression choice should be explicit so players know they are selecting a major endgame reward, not receiving a passive footnote.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your weapon accuracy, damage, and stealth options
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves key ranger spells and class utilities
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you still need staying power and better Concentration checks
